author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XLVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title of this Psalm is, “A song of praise, to the sons of Korah, on the second day of the week.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Concerning this what the Lord deigneth to grant receive ye like sons of the firmament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on the second day of the week, that is, the day after the first which we call the Lord’s day, which also is called the second week-day, was made the firmament of Heaven.…The second day of the week then we ought not to understand but of the Church of Christ: but the Church of Christ in the Saints, the Church of Christ in those who are written in Heaven, the Church of Christ in those who to this world’s temptations yield not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are worthy of the name of “firmament.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church of Christ, then, in those who are strong, of whom saith the Apostle, “We that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak,” is called the firmament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this it is sung in this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear, acknowledge, associate, glory, reign.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Her called firmament, hear also in the Apostolic Epistles, “the pillar and firmament of the truth.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1).…That is, “in the city of our God, in His holy mountain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the city set upon an hill, which cannot be hid: this is the candle which is not hidden under a bushel, to all known, to all proclaimed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet are not all men citizens thereof, but they in whom “great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is that city: let us see whether perhaps, since it is said, “In the city of our God, in His holy mountain,” we ought not to enquire for this mountain where also we may be heard.…What then is that mountain, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One is it with great care to be enquired for, with great solicitude investigated, with labour also to be occupied and ascended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if in any part of the earth it is, what shall we do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we go abroad out of our own country, that to that mountain we may arrive?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, then we are abroad, when in it we are not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that is our city, if we are members of the King, who is the head of the same city.…For there was a certain corner-stone contemptible, whereat the Jews stumbled, cut out of a certain mountain without hands, that is, coming of the kingdom of the Jews without hands, because human operation went not with Mary of whom was born Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if that stone, when the Jews stumbled thereat, had remained there, thou hadst not had whither to ascend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what was done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What saith the prophecy of Daniel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What but that the stone grew, and became a great mountain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that it filled the whole face of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By growing, then, and by filling the whole face of the earth, that mountain came to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then seek we the mountain as though absent, and not as being present ascend to it; that in us the Lord may be “great, and greatly to be praised”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Further,…when he had said, “in the city of our God, in His holy mountain,” what added he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Spreading abroad the joys of the whole earth, the mountains of Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sion is one mountain, why then “mountains”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it that to Sion belonged also those which came from the other side, so as to meet together on the Corner Stone, and become two walls, as it were two mountains, one of the circumcision, the other of the uncircumcision; one of the Jews, the other of the Gentiles: no longer adverse, although diverse, because from different sides, now in the corner not even diverse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He is our peace, who hath made both one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same Corner Stone “which the builders rejected, is become the Head Stone of the corner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The mountain hath joined in itself two mountains; one house there is, and two houses; two, because coming from different sides; one, because of the Corner Stone, wherein both are joined together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear also this, “the mountains of Sion: the sides of the North are the city of the great King.”…See the Gentiles; “the sides of the North:” the sides of the North are joined to the city of the great King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The North is wont to be contrary to Sion: Sion forsooth is in the South, the North over against the South.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is the North, but He who said, “I will sit in the sides of the North, I will be like the Most High”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil had held dominion over the ungodly, and possessed the nations serving images, adoring demons; and all whatsoever there was of human kind anywhere throughout the world, by cleaving to Him, had become North.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since He who binds the strong man, taketh away his goods, and maketh them His own goods; men delivered from infidelity and superstition of devils, believing in Christ, are fitted on to that city, have met in the corner that wall that cometh from the circumcision, and that was made the city of the great King, which had been the sides of the North.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore also in another Scripture is it said, “Out of the North come clouds of golden colour: great is the glory and honour of the Almighty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For great is the glory of the physician, when from being despaired of the sick recovers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Out of the North come clouds,” and not black clouds, not dark clouds, not lowering, but “of golden colour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence but by grace illumined through Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See, “the sides of the North are the city of the great King.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Psalm then follow, and say, “God shall be known in her houses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now in her “houses,” because of the mountains, because of the two walls, because of the two sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God shall be known in her houses,” but he commendeth grace, therefore he added, “when He shall take her up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what would that city have been, unless He had taken her up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would it not immediately have fallen, unless it had such foundation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “other foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none then glory in his own merits; but “he that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.”…The Lord then hath taken up this city, and is known therein, that is, His grace is known in that city: for whatever that city hath, which glorieth in the Lord, it hath not of itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For because of this it is said, “What hast thou that thou didst not receive?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For, lo, the kings of the earth are gathered together” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold now those sides of the North, see how they come, see how they say, “Come ye, and let us go up to the mountain of the Lord: and He will teach us His way, and we will walk in it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And have come together in one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what one, but that “corner-stone”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They saw it, and so they marvelled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After their marvelling at the miracles and glory of Christ, what followed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They were troubled, they were moved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5), “trembling took hold upon them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence took trembling hold upon them, but from the consciousness of sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them run then, king after a king; kings, let them acknowledge the King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore saith He elsewhere, “Yet have I been set by Him a King upon His holy hill of Sion.”…A King then was heard of, set up in Sion, to Him were delivered possessions even to the uttermost parts of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Kings behoved to fear lest they should lose the kingdom, lest the kingdom be taken from them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As wretched Herod feared, and for the Child slew the children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But fearing to lose his kingdom, he deserved not to know the King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would that he too had adored the King with the Magi: not by ill-seeking the kingdom, slain the Innocents, and perished guilty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as concerning him, he destroyed the Innocents: but as for Christ, even a Child, the children dying for Him did He crown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore behoved kings to fear when it was said, “Yet have I been set a King by Him upon His holy hill of Sion,” and inheritance to the uttermost parts of the earth shall He give Him, who set Him up King.…Thence also this is said to them, “Understand now therefore, O ye kings; be instructed, ye judges of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Serve the Lord with fear, and rejoice unto Him with trembling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what did they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There pains as of a woman in travail.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the pains “as of a woman in travail,” but the pangs of a penitent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See the same conception of pain and travail: “Of Thy fear” (saith Isaiah) “we have conceived, we have travailed of the Spirit of salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then the kings conceived from the fear of Christ, that by travailing they brought forth salvation by believing on Him whom they had feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There pains as of a woman in travail:” when of travail thou hearest, expect a birth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The old man travaileth, but the new man is born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With a strong wind Thou shalt break the ships of Tarshish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Briefly understood, this is, Thou shalt overthrow the pride of the nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But where in this history is mentioned the overthrowing of the pride of the nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because of “the ships of Tarshish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Learned men have enquired for Tarshish a city, that is, what city was signified by this name: and to some it has seemed that Cilicia is called Tarshish, because its metropolis is called Tarsus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of which city was the Apostle Paul, being born in Tarsus of Cilicia.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But some have understood by it Carthage, being haply sometimes so named, or in some language so signified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the Prophet Isaiah it is thus found: “Howl, ye ships of Carthage.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in Ezekiel by some interpreters the word is translated Carthage, by some Tarshish: and from this diversity it can be understood that the same which was called Carthage, is called Tharsus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is manifest, that in the beginning of its reign Carthage flourished with ships, and so flourished, that among other nations they excelled in trafficking and navigation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when Dido, flying from her brother, escaped to the parts of Africa, where she built Carthage, the ships which had been prepared for commerce in his country she had taken with her for her flight, the princes of the country consenting to it; and the same ships also when Carthage was built failed not in traffic.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And hence that city became too proud, so that justly by its ships may be understood the pride of the nations, presuming on things uncertain, as on the breath of the winds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now let none presume on full sails, and on the seeming fair state of this life, as of the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be our foundation in Sion: there ought we to be stablished, not to be “carried about with every wind of doctrine.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso then by the uncertain things of this life had been puffed up, let them be overthrown, and be all the pride of the nations subjected to Christ, who shall “with a strong wind break all the ships of Tarshish:” not of any city, but of “Tarshish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How “with a strong wind”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With very strong fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so all pride feared Him that shall judge, as on Him humble to believe, lest Him exalted it should fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As we have heard, so have we seen” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessed Church! at one time thou hast heard, at another time thou hast seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She heard in promises, seeth in performance: heard in Prophecy, seeth in the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all things which are now fulfilled were before prophesied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lift up thine eyes then, and stretch them over the world; see now His “inheritance even to the uttermost parts of the earth:” see now is fulfilled what was said, “All kings shall fall down before Him: all nations shall serve Him:” see fulfilled what was said, “Be Thou exalted, O God, above the heavens, and Thy glory above all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See Him whose feet and hands were pierced with nails, whose bones hanging on the tree were counted, upon whose vesture lots were cast: see reigning whom they saw hanging; see sitting in Heaven whom they despised walking on earth: see thus fulfilled, “All the ends of the earth shall remember, and turn to the Lord, and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seeing all this, exclaim with joy, “As we have heard, so have we seen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly the Church herself is so called out of the Gentiles.…They to whom the Prophets were not sent, first heard and understood the Prophets: they who first heard not, afterwards hearing marvelled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They remained behind to whom they were sent, carrying the books, understanding not the truth: having the tables of the Testament, and not holding the inheritance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we,…“As we have heard, so have we seen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where hearest thou? where seest thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the city of the Lord of Hosts, in the city of our God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath founded it for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not heretics insult, divided into parties, let them not exalt themselves who say, “Lo, here is Christ, or lo, there.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso saith, “Lo, here is Christ, or lo, there,” inviteth to parties.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unity God promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The kings are gathered together in one, not dissipated through schisms.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But haply that city which hath held the world, shall sometime be overthrown?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far be the thought!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God hath founded it for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then God hath founded it for ever, why fearest thou lest the firmament should fall?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We have received Thy mercy, O God, in the midst of Thy people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who have received, and where received?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath not the same Thy people received Thy mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If Thy people hath received Thy mercy, how then, “in the midst of Thy people”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if they who received were one party, they in the midst of whom they received another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great mystery, but yet well known.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When hence also, that is, out of these verses, hath been extracted and brought forth what ye know; it will be not ruder, but sweeter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now forsooth all are reckoned the people of God, who carry His Sacraments, but not all belong to His Mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All forsooth receiving the Sacrament of the Baptism of Christ, are called Christians, but not all live worthily of that Sacrament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are some of whom saith the Apostle, “Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet on account of this form of godliness they are named among God’s people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As to the floor, until the corn is threshed, belongs not the wheat only, but the chaff.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But will it also belong to the garner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the midst then of an evil people is a good people, which hath received the Mercy of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He liveth worthily of the Mercy of God who heareth, and holdeth, and doeth what the Apostle saith, “We beseech you that ye receive not the Grace of God in vain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso then receiveth not the Grace of God in vain, the same receiveth not only the Sacrament, but also the Mercy of God as well.…So those who have the Sacraments, and have not good manners, are both said to be of God, and not of God; are both said to be His, and to be strangers: His because of His own Sacraments, strangers because of their own vice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also strange daughters: daughters, because of the form of godliness; strange, because of their loss of virtue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be the lily there; let it receive the Mercy of God: hold fast the root of a good flower, be not ungrateful for soft rain coming from heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be thorns ungrateful, let them grow by the showers: for the fire they grow, not for the garner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the midst of Thy people not receiving Thy mercy, we have received Thy mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “He came unto His own, and His own received Him not,” yet, in the midst of them, “as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become the sons of God.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he had said, “We have received Thy mercy in the midst of Thy people,” he signified that there is a people not receiving the mercy of God, in the midst of whom some do receive the mercy of God: and then lest it should occur to men that there are so few, as to be nearly none, how did He console them in the words following?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “According to Thy Name, O God, so is Thy praise unto the ends of the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this? …That is, as Thou art known through all the earth, so Thou art also praised through all the earth, nor are there wanting who now praise Thee through all the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they praise Thee who live well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “According to Thy Name, O God, so is Thy praise,” not in a part, but “unto the ends of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy right hand is full of righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, many are they also who shall stand at Thy right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only shall they be many who shall stand at Thy left hand, but there also shall be a full heap set at Thy right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let mount Zion rejoice, and the daughters of Judah be glad, because of Thy judgments, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O mount Zion, O daughters of Judah, ye labour now among tares, among chaff, among thorns ye labour: yet be glad because of God’s judgments.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God erreth not in judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Live ye separate, though separate ye were not born; not vainly hath a voice gone forth from your mouth and heart, “Destroy not my soul with sinners, nor my life with bloody men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shall winnow with such art, carrying in His hand a fan, that not one grain of wheat shall fall into the heap of chaff prepared to be burned, nor one beard of chaff pass to the heap to be laid up in the garner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be glad, O ye daughters of Judæa, because of the judgments of God that erreth not, and do not yet judge rashly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To you let it belong to collect, to Him let it belong to separate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But think not that the “daughters of Judah” are Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Judah is confession; all the sons of confession are all the sons of Judah.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “salvation is of the Jews,” is nothing else than that Christ is of the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This saith also the Apostle, “He is not a Jew which is one outwardly; neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh: but he is a Jew which is one inwardly, and circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit, and not in the letter, whose praise is not of men, but of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be such a Jew; glory in the circumcision of the heart, though thou hast not the circumcision of the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of Thy judgments, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Walk about Zion, and embrace her” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be it said to them who live ill, in the midst of whom is the people, which hath received the mercy of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the midst of you is a people living well, “Walk about Zion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how? “embrace her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not with scandals, but with love go round about her: that so those who live well in the midst of you ye may imitate, and by imitation of them, be incorporate with Christ, whose members they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Walk about Zion, go round about her: speak in the towers thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the height of her bulwarks, set forth the praises thereof.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Set your hearts upon her might” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that ye may have the form of godliness, deny the power thereof, but, “upon her might set your hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speak ye in her towers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the might of this city?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso would understand the might of this city, let him understand the force of love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is a virtue which none conquereth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love’s flame no waves of the world, no streams of temptation, extinguish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this it is said, “Love is strong as death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as when death cometh, it cannot be resisted; by whatever arts, whatever medicines, you meet it; the violence of death can none avoid who is born mortal; so against the violence of love can the world do nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from the contrary the similitude is made of death; for as death is most violent to take away, so love is most violent to save.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through love many have died to the world, to live to God; by this love inflamed, the martyrs, not pretenders, not puffed up by vain-glory, not such as they of whom it is written, “Though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing,” but men whom truly a love of Christ and of the truth led on to this passion; what to them were the temptations of the tormentors?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Greater violence had the eyes of their weeping friends, than the persecutions of enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how many were held by their children, that they might not suffer? to how many did their wives fall upon their knees, that they might not be left widows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many have their parents forbidden to die; as we know and read in the Passion of the Blessed Perpetua!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All this was done; but tears, however great, and with whatever force flowing, when did they extinguish the ardour of love?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the might of Sion, to whom elsewhere it is said, “Peace be within thy walls, and prosperity within thy palaces.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What here understand we, “Set your hearts upon her might, and distribute her houses”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, distinguish house from house.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not confound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is a house having the form of godliness, and not having godliness; but there is a house having both form and godliness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Distribute, confound not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But then ye distribute and confound not, when ye “set your hearts upon her might;” that is, when through love ye are made spiritual.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then ye will not judge rashly, then ye will see that the evil harms not the good as long as we are in this floor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Distribute her houses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There can be also another understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The two houses, one coming of the circumcision, one of the uncircumcision, it is commanded the Apostles to distribute.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when Saul was called, and made the Apostle Paul, agreeing in unity with his fellow Apostles, he so with thorn determined, that they should go to the circumcision, he to the uncircumcision.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By that dispensation of their Apostleship, they distributed the houses of the city of the great King; and meeting in the corner, divided the Gospel in dispensation, in love united it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And truly this is rather to be understood; for it followeth and showeth that it is here said to the preachers, “distribute her houses: that ye may tell it to the generation following:” that is, that even to us, who were to come after them, their dispensation of the Gospel should reach: For not for those only they laboured, with whom they lived in the earth; nor the Lord for those Apostles only to whom He deigned to show Himself alive after His Resurrection, but for us also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to them He spake, and signified us when He spake, “Lo, I am with you alway, even to the end of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were they then to be here alway, even to the end of the world?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Also He said, “Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on Me through their word.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore He considereth us, because He suffered on account of us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly then it is said, “That ye may tell it to the generation following.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For this is God, even our God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The earth was seen, the earth’s Creator was not seen; the flesh was held, God in the flesh was not acknowledged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the flesh was held by those from whom had been taken the same flesh, for of the seed of Abraham was the Virgin Mary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At the flesh they stayed, the Divinity they did not understand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Apostles, O mighty city, preach thou on the towers, and say, “This is God, even our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So, even so as He was despised, as He lay a stone before the feet of the stumbling, that He might humble the hearts of the confessing; even so, “This is God, even our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly He was seen, as was said, “Afterward did He show Himself upon earth, and conversed with men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This is God, even our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is also Man, and who is there will know Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This is God, even our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But haply for a time as the false gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For because they can be called gods, but cannot be so, for a time they are even called so.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what saith the Prophet, or what warneth He to be said to them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This shall ye say to them, “The gods that have not made the heavens and the earth, even they shall perish from the earth, and from those that are under the heavens.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is not such a god: for our God is above all gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Above all what gods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For all the gods of the nations are idols, but the Lord made the heavens.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same then is our God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This is God, even our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how long?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For ever and ever: He shall rule us for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He is our God, He is also our King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He protecteth us, being our God, lest we die; He ruleth us, being our King, lest we fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by ruling us He doth not break us; for whom He ruleth not, He breaketh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt rule them,” saith He, “with a rod of iron, and dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are whom He ruleth not; these He spareth not, as a potter’s vessel dashing them in pieces.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By Him then let us wish to be ruled and delivered, “for He is our God for ever and ever, and He shall rule us for ever.”
